title: FIX-489: Approval Status — Pending Approvals Surfaced at Top of List
description: This tour walks through the changes introduced for ticket FIX-489 (Approval status). The core problem was that tickets waiting for peer approval were rendered inline with all other tickets, making them easy to overlook. The fix ensures they appear in a dedicated, prominent section at the top of the ticket list so every team member can see and act on them quickly — either approving or flagging as 'needs defense' to immediately remove them from the approval queue. The tour follows the agent run context files in chronological order, explaining the rationale and progression of the fix across two Helix agent runs.

steps.description: This is the context record for the first Helix agent run (run ID: cmpalyq60007rfd0udul5ahdh) targeting ticket FIX-489. It establishes the working branch (`helix/fix/FIX-489-approval-status`) and confirms the target repository is `helix-cli`. This is the entry point: the agent was instructed to fix the approval status display so that tickets awaiting peer approval no longer get buried in the general list.
steps.title: Overview: First Run Context

steps.description: The Description block defines the product requirement driving this change. Tickets in a 'waiting for peer approval' state must appear in a visually distinct section at the top of the ticket list — not interspersed with other tickets. Reviewers must be able to approve or mark a ticket as 'needs defense' with minimal friction. When flagged as 'needs defense', a ticket should immediately leave the approval queue, keeping the list clean and actionable.
steps.title: Problem Statement: Approval Visibility

steps.description: This file records the second Helix agent run (run ID: cmpbln4ad005ggz0uh7j85duy) on the same ticket and branch. The second run was triggered after the first completed successfully, as part of a follow-up sync. The shared metadata (ticket_id, short_id, branch, repo) confirms continuity with the first run — both runs operated on the same feature branch targeting the same repositories.
steps.title: Second Run: Continuity and Context

steps.description: The Discussion section records the agent's activity log across both runs. The first run completed successfully and updated 2 repositories, with verification screenshots attached. The second run also completed but then encountered a git conflict on push — the remote branch had diverged due to concurrent pushes. This is an infrastructure-level sync issue, not a code logic failure. The actual approval-status changes were already merged by the first run.
steps.title: Discussion Log: Run Outcomes and Git Conflict

steps.description: The Helix agent explains the root cause of the second run's failure: a rejected push due to the remote branch already containing newer commits not reflected locally. This is a housekeeping/sync issue unrelated to the correctness of the approval-status feature. The agent confirms the code changes from the first run were successfully merged and that the second run's failure only affected the trailing sync step.
steps.title: Failure Root Cause: Git Push Conflict Explained

steps.description: This intraview artifact was generated for run cmpbuahhh0024ml0uuxy02t5r. It mirrors the structure of the previous intraview but reflects a later iteration of the tour description — noting that the tour covers changes across 'two runs' in chronological order. This version refines the language around the 'needs defense' workflow, clarifying that flagging a ticket removes it from the approval queue immediately.
steps.title: Intraview Artifact: Run cmpbuahhh0024ml0uuxy02t5r

steps.description: This is the most recent intraview artifact, generated for run cmpbunuws002qml0ufdtgpmxu. It consolidates the tour description with the most complete language: the fix ensures pending-approval tickets surface in a dedicated prominent section at the top of the list, with quick-action controls (approve or needs-defense) visible to all team members. This artifact serves as the canonical walkthrough for the completed feature and confirms all major code paths for approval status handling are documented and clean.
steps.title: Summary: Final Intraview and Code Path Confirmation
